channel.title: Records of the !mmortal Data Scientist
channel.description: Notes on AI, math, and the long road. Live slow, die whenever.

item.title: Organizing Randomness: Contrastive Learning in JAX
item.description: A block-by-block JAX + Optax implementation of six contrastive losses, each watched as a real animated GIF turning random 2D points into organized embeddings. The runnable companion to "Untangling the Moons."

item.title: What an MLP Knows, When It's a Kernel
item.description: The MLP block is illegible because its primitive does not carry a kernel. Give it one and the four objects that make attention legible — pairwise scores, normalised contributions, named geometry, prototype units — follow for free.

item.title: Attention is Explainable Because it is a Kernel
item.description: A reading of self-attention through kernel smoothing and RKHS.

item.title: Not All Infinities Are Equal
item.description: The singularity structure of cross-entropy explains hallucination, the modality gap, and why contrastive losses need such big batches.

item.title: Opposite Is Not Different
item.description: The cosine-similarity scale has three landmarks, not two. Maximum difference is orthogonality, not opposition — and the most influential contrastive losses spent years optimizing for the wrong target.

item.title: Activations Are Bad for Geometry
item.description: Pointwise activations factor into the layer's Jacobian as a diagonal modulation. The same modulation that buys selectivity destroys geometric structure on the data manifold.

item.title: Morocco and AI Illiteracy — Part I
item.description: On Morocco, AI education, and the cost of staying behind.
